title: Request and Response Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of the breakdown of req properties (body/params/query) and core res methods (json/status/redirect).

questions.question: Which property in the 'req' object is commonly used in Express to access dynamic URL parameters like ':id' (e.g., /users/123)?
Which method in the 'res' object is the industry standard for sending a JavaScript object back to a client as JSON?
Which property in the 'req' object is used to access 'Query Parameters' (the part after the ? in a URL like /search?q=node)?
Which method in the 'res' object would you use to change the current web page the user is viewing to a different URL?
Does standard 'req.body' access require a middleware like 'express.json()' to be used in your application first?
options: Yes, as Express doesn't parse JSON data automatically for performance reasons.
